mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns during periods of falling FEDFUNDS over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals weakening labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and rising consumer prices indicates robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, which forces equity valuations higher as firms raise prices and margins expand over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 55 percent of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate improvements in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which trigger algorithmic buying flows and generate positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO revisions are statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-stat < 1.5) across a 5-year backtest window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in real personal income exceeding 2 percent signals strong household purchasing power, which directly boosts retail and consumer discretionary earnings forecasts and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain negative or flat in 70 percent of months where RSAFS growth surpasses 2 percent, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal generates negative 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered months, or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 200 while the federal funds rate remains below 3 percent, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting investors to rotate into equities as a hedge against currency debasement and driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in over 55 percent of periods meeting the threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52 percent in a 3-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in real GDP and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate revenue expectations across multiple sectors, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The dual demand signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of observations, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.
